full_prompt: Design a 3D-printed cellular lattice inner liner for a bicycle helmet as an open-source explicit-dynamics simulation benchmark. The liner replaces traditional EPS foam with a lattice printed in TPU 95A (hyperelastic, 2nd-order Ogden with C10 ~ 0.4 MPa, density 1200 kg/m^3) occupying the shell between the outer PC shell and the rider's head. The liner must conform to a standard medium adult helmet inner volume with a wall thickness of 25 mm across the cranial region, a nominal mass less than 180 g, and a lattice cell type (gyroid TPMS or BCC / FCC / octet truss) chosen by the designer. The helmet must satisfy the CPSC 1203 / EN 1078 impact test protocol: (LC1) a 5.0 kg headform dropped from 1.2 m onto a flat anvil (impact velocity 4.85 m/s, kinetic energy 58.8 J), (LC2) the same 5.0 kg headform dropped from 1.05 m onto a curbstone anvil (impact velocity 4.54 m/s, kinetic energy 51.5 J), (LC3) a quasi-static roll-off test at 3 kg moment, (LC4) penetration resistance at 3.0 kg pointed striker from 0.3 m. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) peak headform linear acceleration during flat-anvil impact must not exceed 250 g, the CPSC 1203 limit, (R2) peak linear acceleration during curbstone impact must not exceed 250 g per CPSC 1203, (R3) the TPU lattice must absorb at least 85% of the incoming 58.8 J kinetic energy via plastic/hyperelastic deformation without full densification, (R4) liner mass must not exceed 180 g, and (R5) a linear buckling analysis of a representative lattice unit cell under 1.5x flat-impact peak load must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 solid body or lattice-file of the liner plus a verification report with peak linear acceleration for flat and curbstone impacts, energy absorbed, liner mass, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a 3D-printed TPU 95A lattice inner liner for a bicycle helmet, replacing conventional EPS foam. The liner occupies the shell between the outer PC shell and the rider's head, conforming to a medium adult helmet inner volume with a ~25 mm wall thickness across the cranial region. Designer chooses the lattice cell type (gyroid TPMS, BCC, FCC, or octet truss). TPU 95A is hyperelastic (Ogden).

Design to CPSC 1203 / EN 1078. The headform is a rigid mass seating against the liner between the outer PC shell and the head.

Load cases.
- LC1 flat-anvil drop: 5.0 kg headform at 1.2 m (4.85 m/s, 58.8 J).
- LC2 curbstone drop: 5.0 kg headform at 1.05 m (4.54 m/s, 51.5 J).
- LC3 roll-off: quasi-static 3 kg moment.
- LC4 penetration: 3.0 kg pointed striker from 0.3 m.

Liner mass target is <=180 g; also check unit-cell buckling at 1.5x flat-impact peak load.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Lattice-cell effective stiffness and energy-density curves are pre-computed by homogenization and fed to the full-helmet explicit-dynamic FEA.

prompt.title: TPU lattice bicycle helmet liner (explicit-dynamics benchmark)
prompt.brief: Design a TPU 95A 3D-printed lattice inner liner (25 mm wall, <=180 g) for a bicycle helmet passing CPSC 1203 / EN 1078 flat and curbstone 250 g peak impact, 85% energy absorption, and 2x buckling margin.

boundary_conditions.headform: rigid mass; liner seats between outer PC shell and headform

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Retention strap tensile test per CPSC 1203
